codeReview.summary: The original feature commit did not fully satisfy the contract, but the follow-up fix commit `a28628b` resolves the prior blocking gaps for polygon mesh visibility and hatch spline handling in the current repository state. The current bridge preserves polyline family identity on both sides, keeps polygon mesh dimensions visible enough for compat enumeration, preserves hatch metadata/path structure for supported edge types, and no longer panics when compat hatch paths contain spline edges.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `dxf-bridge-worker` skill still advertises `cargo test --lib native_bridge`, but this workspace has no root library target; both the original worker handoff and the repo's services config use `cargo test --bin H7CAD native_bridge -- --test-threads=16` instead.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: D:/work/plant-code/cad/H7CAD/.factory/skills/dxf-bridge-worker/SKILL.md step 8 references `cargo test --lib native_bridge`, while D:/work/plant-code/cad/H7CAD/.factory/services.yaml defines `test_bridge: cargo test --bin H7CAD native_bridge -- --test-threads=16`, and the worker transcript/handoff for session 79b1291b-b0ac-4e4e-92ac-d096142409a8 notes the `--lib` command failed with `no library targets found`.

summary: Review passed against the current post-fix repository state. The original bridge feature had mesh-structure and hatch-spline defects, but the follow-up fix feature `bridge-polyline-hatch-structure-fixes` addressed those failures, so no remaining blocking issue was found for `bridge-polyline-and-hatch` in the current implementation.
